basics.name: Geoffrey Miller
basics.label: Sr Engineering Manager, SRE
basics.summary: Infrastructure Director and Principal Cloud Architect with 12 years designing and operating cloud platforms at regulated FinTechs (20 years in IT). Builds high-performing platform teams, manages stakeholders across business and technology, and ships simple repeatable engineering patterns that cut spend 30%+ while tightening compliance. Advanced leader in agentic software engineering — ships controlled agentic layers using MCP servers, patterned agents, and automated verification with competing incentives.

work.name: PayPal
highlights: Lead and specialize in agentic software engineering — mostly-automated development with controlled approval gates.
Ship agentic code and configuration review tools with automatic approval-recommendation gates for the Command Center.
Built real-time incident management and collaboration tooling that speeds up live P0/P1 incident recovery.
Built a risk inventory system mapping mitigation capabilities and auditing reliability readiness across all PayPal brands.
Authored GameDay tooling that ingests incident logs and chat transcripts to generate agentically-scored readiness feedback.
Recognized as a top-impact AI user enterprise-wide; appointed to PayPal's AI Super Users community.
Consult across all PayPal brands on cloud architecture, DevOps, and reliability engineering.

work.name: First Citizens Bank
highlights: Architected and delivered a secure multi-account AWS Landing Zone (100+ accounts) with centralized logging, IAM federation, and policy-as-code guardrails enabling 30+ product teams.
Led FinOps program; reduced AWS spend 30% year-over-year via dashboards, automated rightsizing, and savings-plan optimization.
Replaced thousands of duplicate VPC interface endpoints with a single multi-AZ endpoint hub — a top-3 line item → negligible.
Designed hybrid connectivity with AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, and Megaport for sub-5ms latency.
Guided the team through the Silicon Valley Bank → First Citizens Bank integration, including platform migrations.
Led and mentored a 12-member cloud platform engineering team; founded the Cloud Community of Practice.
Enforced continuous FFIEC, PCI-DSS, and SOC 2 compliance via policy-as-code gates (Sentinel, OPA) integrated into CI/CD.
Engineered bank-hardened EKS platform — multi-AZ, CIS-hardened, OPA/Gatekeeper, Karpenter, automated zero-day patching.
Decomposed monolithic repo into team-owned repos with CODEOWNERS and mandatory gates; lead time 6–12 weeks → days.
Served on the bank's AI governance board — crafted secure onboarding patterns for GenAI/ML tools.

work.name: REPAY (Realtime Electronic Payments)
highlights: Built green-field AWS platform with Terraform, Packer, and Kubernetes; passed PCI-DSS audit with zero critical findings.
Launched Backstage Internal Developer Platform and Documentation-as-Code; cut documentation drift 75%.
Grew infrastructure and SRE organization from 1 engineer to 18 across SRE, ops, and platform development.
Established FinOps automation cutting AWS spend 30% year-over-year.
Built reusable landing-zone patterns reducing cloud migration onboarding from months to a single day.
Deployed Prometheus / Loki / Grafana observability stack with 24/7 PagerDuty on-call.
Built secure EKS with GitOps, admission control, and multi-dimensional autoscaling.

work.name: Early Warning Services (Zelle)
highlights: DevOps engineering at Early Warning Services — the bank-owned network that operates Zelle — during the launch period.
Built immutable CI/CD pipelines and containerized microservices supporting daily releases.
Automated blue/green deployments and A/B testing to reduce deployment risk.

work.name: Thinkvine
highlights: Migrated Windows and Linux workloads to AWS, converting legacy infrastructure to Terraform IaC with CI/CD.

work.name: OWNZONES Media Network
highlights: Designed green-field AWS infrastructure for video workloads using S3 lifecycle policies and Glacier archival to optimize storage cost.

work.name: State of Arizona, Dept. of Administration
highlights: Introduced Git source control and automated unit testing to mainframe COBOL systems.
